subtopics.title: Bar Chart
subtopics.explanation: A bar chart displays categorical data with rectangular bars where bar length is proportional to the value. Ideal for comparing quantities across categories.
subtopics.example: Comparing sales across different product categories: electronics, clothing, groceries.
subtopics.code:
df.groupby('category')['sales'].sum().plot(kind='bar')
plt.title('Sales by Category')
plt.ylabel('Total Sales')
plt.show()

subtopics.title: Line Chart
subtopics.explanation: A line chart displays information as a series of data points connected by straight lines. Perfect for showing trends over time or continuous data.
subtopics.example: Plotting monthly website traffic over a year to identify seasonal patterns and growth.
subtopics.code:
plt.plot(df['date'], df['traffic'])
plt.xlabel('Date')
plt.ylabel('Website Traffic')
plt.title('Traffic Trend Over Time')
plt.show()

subtopics.title: Scatter Plot
subtopics.explanation: A scatter plot uses Cartesian coordinates to display values for two variables. It reveals correlations, clusters, and outliers in the relationship between variables.
subtopics.example: Plotting advertising spend vs. sales revenue shows if increased spending leads to higher sales.
subtopics.code:
plt.scatter(df['ad_spend'], df['revenue'])
plt.xlabel('Ad Spend')
plt.ylabel('Revenue')
plt.title('Ad Spend vs Revenue')
plt.show()
